input_text: MRT selalu delay pagi2 ni. Dah la nak kena punchcard pukul 8. Kerajaan kena tambah baik servis pengangkutan awam kalau nak orang tinggalkan kereta.
reason: Constructive complaint about public transport delays. Criticism of service quality is legitimate civic feedback.

input_text: Harga ayam naik lagi. Bulan lepas RM9 sekarang dah RM11 sekilo. Macam mana nak masak untuk family besar ni? Kerajaan tolong kawal sikit harga barang.
reason: Respectful complaint about rising food prices with a call for government price controls. Standard consumer concern.

input_text: Saya rasa dasar pendidikan STEM kita masih kurang berkesan. Banyak sekolah luar bandar tak ada lab sains yang lengkap. Perlu lebih banyak peruntukan.
reason: Neutral critique of STEM education policy with constructive suggestion. Academic discussion of public policy.

input_text: Toll naik, minyak naik, gaji tak naik. Ini realiti rakyat Malaysia hari ini. Harap kerajaan dengar suara kami.
reason: Expression of economic frustration directed at government policy. Legitimate civic complaint without incitement.

input_text: Sistem peperiksaan SPM patut dikaji semula. Bukan semua pelajar boleh perform dalam exam format. Ada yang lebih bagus dalam projek dan hands-on.
reason: Constructive critique of Malaysia's examination system suggesting alternative assessment methods.

input_text: Saya sokong hak pekerja untuk berdemonstrasi secara aman. Itu hak asasi yang dijamin Perlembagaan. Selagi tak ganas, tak ada masalah.
reason: Supports the constitutional right to peaceful assembly. Explicitly conditioned on non-violence.

input_text: Kesian tengok longkang tersumbat depan rumah dah 3 bulan tak ada tindakan dari majlis perbandaran. Report dah banyak kali tapi tak ada response.
reason: Complaint about local council service failure. Standard civic grievance about infrastructure maintenance.

input_text: Pendapat saya, SPRM kena diberi lebih kuasa untuk siasat kes rasuah tanpa perlu kelulusan AG. Baru betul-betul bebas.
reason: Constructive opinion about strengthening anti-corruption enforcement. Policy discussion within lawful bounds.

input_text: Subsidi minyak tu memang perlu dikaji semula, tapi pastikan golongan B40 tak terjejas. Subsidi bersasar lebih adil.
reason: Balanced discussion of subsidy rationalization with concern for vulnerable groups. Constructive policy debate.

input_text: Apa kata kita semua hormat satu sama lain masa Ramadan? Bukan susah pun. Orang puasa, kita jangan makan depan dia secara sengaja. Basic courtesy je.
reason: Promotes interfaith courtesy during Ramadan. Encourages mutual respect across communities.

input_text: Keputusan mahkamah tu kena hormati walaupun kita tak setuju. Kalau tak puas hati, ada saluran rayuan. Itu namanya rule of law.
reason: Respects judicial process and rule of law while acknowledging right to disagree. Supports constitutional order.

input_text: Saya harap kerajaan negeri boleh tingkatkan pengurusan sampah. Kawasan saya dah penuh tong sampah melimpah setiap minggu.
reason: Routine civic complaint about waste management. Directed at improving public services.

input_text: Dasar kuota universiti memang satu topik sensitif, tapi kita kena bincang secara terbuka supaya semua pihak faham perspektif masing-masing.
reason: Calls for open dialogue on sensitive quota policy without stereotyping any group. Promotes constructive discourse.

input_text: Kalau nak ekonomi maju, kena invest dalam R&D. Malaysia belanja kurang dari 1% GDP untuk penyelidikan, jauh ketinggalan berbanding Korea Selatan.
reason: Factual economic policy discussion comparing Malaysia's R&D spending to other countries. Constructive suggestion.

input_text: Saya rasa bagus kerajaan promote Malaysia sebagai hub halal global. Tapi kena pastikan standard betul-betul dijaga, bukan sekadar label.
reason: Supportive of halal economy initiative with constructive caveat about maintaining standards.

input_text: Jalan raya depan sekolah anak saya berlubang teruk. Dah 6 bulan buat aduan, masih sama. Harap JKR boleh ambil tindakan segera.
reason: Specific complaint about road infrastructure near a school. Standard public service complaint.

input_text: Saya tak setuju dengan kenyataan menteri tu pasal kos sara hidup. Realiti di bawah jauh berbeza dengan statistik yang diorang tunjuk.
reason: Disagrees with a minister's statement on cost of living. Legitimate political criticism without personal attacks.

input_text: Cuaca panas gila sekarang. Harap kerajaan ada plan jangka panjang untuk tackle perubahan iklim. Jangan tunggu sampai air habis baru nak bertindak.
reason: Concerns about climate change and call for government action. Environmental advocacy is legitimate.

input_text: Rakyat berhak tahu macam mana duit cukai kita dibelanjakan. Transparency dalam kerajaan bukan pilihan, itu kewajipan.
reason: Calls for government transparency and fiscal accountability. Fundamental civic demand.

input_text: Baguslah kalau boleh tambah bas awam kat kawasan luar bandar. Anak-anak nak pergi sekolah pun susah kalau tak ada transport.
reason: Request for improved rural public transportation, especially for schoolchildren. Constructive civic suggestion.

input_text: Kita patut belajar dari negara lain macam mana urus sisa pepejal. Jepun contoh yang baik untuk kitar semula.
reason: Suggests learning from other countries on waste management. Constructive environmental policy discussion.

input_text: Penat la asyik dengar janji manifesto tapi tak nampak pelaksanaan. Rakyat dah pandai menilai sekarang, tak boleh tipu lagi.
reason: Political frustration about unfulfilled manifesto promises. Legitimate voter sentiment without incitement.

input_text: Hospital kerajaan kena tambah doktor pakar. Appointment jumpa specialist pun kena tunggu 6 bulan. Yang mampu je boleh pergi swasta.
reason: Critique of public healthcare wait times and inequality of access. Constructive health policy concern.

input_text: Saya harap dialog antara agama terus diperkukuhkan. Kalau kita tak faham satu sama lain, mudah jadi salah faham.
reason: Promotes interfaith dialogue and mutual understanding. Strongly aligned with harmony principles.

input_text: Kenapa gaji minimum masih rendah berbanding kos hidup di KL? Pekerja makan gaji memang struggle. Kerajaan kena review balik.
reason: Critique of minimum wage relative to cost of living. Standard labor policy discussion.

input_text: Bagus DBKL buat taman baru kat kawasan flat. Anak-anak perlukan ruang bermain yang selamat. Teruskan usaha macam ni.
reason: Positive feedback about local council creating parks. Appreciative civic engagement.

input_text: PRU dah dekat, semua parti mula berkempen. Yang penting rakyat buat pilihan berdasarkan fakta, bukan emosi.
reason: Neutral election commentary encouraging fact-based voting. Supports democratic process.

input_text: Saya rasa kita perlu lebih banyak sekolah vokasional. Tak semua anak minat akademik, dan tak ada salahnya jadi tukang yang mahir.
reason: Advocates for vocational education as a valid career path. Constructive education policy suggestion.

input_text: Air paip kawasan saya selalu keruh lepas hujan lebat. Syabas kena improve loji rawatan air supaya kualiti terjamin.
reason: Complaint about water quality and call for utility improvement. Basic public service feedback.

input_text: Masalah banjir KL ni bukan baru. Setiap tahun sama je. Kena ada master plan yang betul untuk saliran air bandar.
reason: Recurring flood problem critique with constructive call for proper urban drainage planning.

input_text: Polis trafik patut lebih kerap buat operasi kat highway. Pemandu lori yang memandu bahaya dah jadi ancaman keselamatan jalan raya.
reason: Calls for better traffic enforcement for road safety. Legitimate public safety concern.

input_text: Sukan SEA Games baru lepas, Malaysia boleh la perform. Tapi kita kena invest lebih dalam sukan akar umbi, bukan masa nak dekat games baru sibuk.
reason: Sports commentary with constructive criticism of grassroots investment in athletics.

input_text: Saya hormat semua agama. Deepavali, Hari Raya, CNY, Christmas - semua ni peluang kita raikan kepelbagaian Malaysia.
reason: Celebrates Malaysia's multireligious festivals and diversity. Strongly promotes harmony.

input_text: Internet kat kampung saya sangat perlahan. Streamyx zaman bila lagi ni. Kerajaan kata nak digital economy tapi infrastruktur basic pun tak settle.
reason: Criticism of rural internet infrastructure gap. Legitimate digital divide concern.

input_text: Taman Negara kena dijaga. Jangan bagi pembalakan haram rosakkan hutan kita. Itu warisan untuk generasi akan datang.
reason: Environmental conservation advocacy. Calls for protection of national forests.

input_text: The government needs to seriously look into public hospital waiting times. My mum waited 8 hours at the emergency department last week. That's not acceptable.
reason: Personal account criticizing public healthcare wait times. Legitimate complaint about government services.

input_text: I think Malaysia's anti-corruption efforts are still not strong enough. We need independent oversight, not just more laws on paper.
reason: Constructive critique of anti-corruption framework calling for institutional reform. Policy discussion.

input_text: Honestly the MRT has been a game changer for KL commuters. But they really need to extend it to more areas, especially PJ and Subang.
reason: Balanced feedback on MRT - acknowledges improvement while suggesting expansion. Constructive civic input.

input_text: The court ruling on that land case was disappointing but we have to respect the judicial process. There's always the appeals court.
reason: Expresses disagreement with a court ruling while respecting rule of law and due process.

input_text: Why are we still debating whether to teach science and maths in English or BM? Just give parents the choice and let schools offer both streams.
reason: Proposes a pragmatic solution to the language-in-education debate. Constructive policy suggestion.

input_text: Food delivery riders deserve better protections. No insurance, no EPF, working in rain and heat. The gig economy shouldn't mean zero worker rights.
reason: Advocates for gig worker rights and social protections. Legitimate labor rights discussion.

input_text: The subsidy for RON95 petrol should be targeted at those who actually need it. Why should someone driving a BMW get the same subsidy as a Myvi owner?
reason: Argues for targeted fuel subsidies based on need. Standard economic policy debate.

input_text: I've lived in Malaysia my whole life and I love how we celebrate each other's festivals. My Malay neighbour always sends kuih during Raya and we share mooncakes during Mid-Autumn.
reason: Positive personal account of intercultural harmony and neighborly relations in Malaysia.

input_text: Peaceful protests are a democratic right. As long as people don't destroy property or block emergency vehicles, they should be allowed to assemble.
reason: Supports right to peaceful assembly with reasonable conditions. Aligns with constitutional rights.

input_text: Malaysia's brain drain is real. Young graduates are leaving for Singapore because salaries here can't keep up with cost of living. We need to fix this.
reason: Discusses brain drain problem with constructive concern. Legitimate economic and talent policy critique.

input_text: The quota system in university admissions needs a proper review. We should have a transparent discussion about how to ensure fairness for everyone.
reason: Calls for transparent review of university quota policy without stereotyping any ethnic group.

input_text: Climate change is hitting us hard - floods in December, water rationing in March. We need a proper climate adaptation plan, not just reacting to emergencies.
reason: Environmental policy critique calling for proactive climate planning. Factual and constructive.

input_text: E-hailing regulation should protect both drivers and passengers. Right now the companies take too big a cut and safety standards aren't consistent.
reason: Balanced critique of e-hailing regulation affecting both drivers and passengers. Consumer and labor concern.

input_text: I wish local councils would actually maintain public parks properly. The playground near my house has been broken for months and nobody fixes it.
reason: Complaint about local council maintenance of public facilities. Standard civic grievance.

input_text: Malaysia's renewable energy targets are ambitious but we're behind schedule. Solar panels on every government building would be a good start.
reason: Constructive critique of renewable energy progress with a practical suggestion.

input_text: The opposition plays an important role in our democracy. Without checks and balances, any government can become complacent.
reason: Supports democratic checks and balances. Affirms the legitimate role of political opposition.

input_text: Property prices in KL are insane. A young couple earning average salary cannot afford even a small apartment. The government should build more affordable housing.
reason: Criticism of housing affordability with constructive call for government action. Common civic concern.

input_text: Election debates should be mandatory for all candidates. Voters deserve to hear their MP candidates answer tough questions directly.
reason: Advocates for greater electoral transparency through candidate debates. Pro-democratic suggestion.

input_text: I support freedom of the press in Malaysia. Journalists should be able to report without fear. A free media makes our democracy stronger.
reason: Advocates for press freedom as a pillar of democracy. Constructive civic position.

input_text: Our national football team has potential but the management of FAM needs a total overhaul. Too much politics in sports.
reason: Criticism of football association management. Sports commentary with call for institutional reform.

input_text: Mental health services in government clinics are severely underfunded. One psychiatrist for thousands of patients is not sustainable.
reason: Highlights mental health service gaps in public healthcare. Constructive health policy critique.

input_text: 马来西亚的公共交通还需要很大的改善。每天塞车浪费太多时间了，政府应该加快建设轻轨网络。
reason: Critique of Malaysian public transport and call for faster LRT expansion. Constructive civic feedback in Chinese.

input_text: 槟城的物价越来越高，尤其是房价。年轻人根本买不起房子，希望政府能推出更多可负担房屋计划。
reason: Concerns about Penang housing affordability for young people. Standard cost-of-living complaint.

input_text: 我觉得马来西亚的多元文化是我们最大的优势。华人新年、开斋节、屠妖节，大家一起庆祝，这就是马来西亚的美。
reason: Celebrates Malaysia's multicultural festivals as a national strength. Promotes intercultural harmony.

input_text: 政府应该加强反贪污力度。不管是哪个党执政，贪污都不能被容忍。希望SPRM能更加独立地运作。
reason: Calls for stronger anti-corruption measures and MACC independence. Non-partisan policy critique.

input_text: 马来西亚的华文教育体系需要得到更多支持。独中为国家培养了很多人才，政府应该承认统考文凭。
reason: Advocates for recognition of Chinese independent school qualifications. Education policy discussion.

input_text: 油价又涨了。虽然我理解补贴合理化的必要性，但政府必须确保低收入群体不会受到太大影响。
reason: Balanced view on fuel price increase acknowledging need for subsidy reform while protecting the poor.

input_text: 选举快到了，希望大家理性投票，不要被假消息影响。每一票都很重要，这是我们的民主权利。
reason: Encourages rational voting and warns against misinformation. Supports democratic participation.

input_text: 我支持和平集会的权利。只要不暴力，人民有权表达不满。这是宪法赋予的自由。
reason: Supports constitutional right to peaceful assembly. Explicitly conditioned on non-violence.

input_text: 马来西亚的医疗系统其实不错，但是政府医院太拥挤了。等专科门诊要等好几个月，这需要改善。
reason: Acknowledges strengths of Malaysian healthcare while critiquing overcrowding. Balanced health policy feedback.

input_text: 本地的环保意识还不够强。垃圾分类应该强制执行，不能只是说说而已。看看日本和台湾怎么做的。
reason: Environmental advocacy calling for mandatory recycling. Constructive comparison with other countries.

input_text: 斋月期间，我虽然不是穆斯林，但我尊重朋友的信仰。大家互相理解才是马来西亚精神。
reason: Non-Muslim expressing respect for Ramadan and interfaith understanding. Promotes social harmony.

input_text: 网络在乡下地区真的很慢。政府说要数字经济，但基础设施跟不上有什么用？先把网速搞好再说。
reason: Criticism of rural digital infrastructure gap. Legitimate concern about digital divide.

input_text: 大学录取的固打制度需要公开讨论。应该找到一个对所有族群都公平的方案，而不是回避这个话题。
reason: Calls for open discussion on university quota system without targeting any ethnic group. Promotes fair dialogue.

input_text: 法院的判决我们可以不同意，但必须尊重司法程序。不满意可以上诉，这才是法治精神。
reason: Respects judicial process and rule of law while acknowledging right to disagree and appeal.

input_text: 希望吉隆坡能多建一些公园和绿地。城市发展不能只看高楼大厦，居民也需要休闲空间。
reason: Advocates for more urban green spaces in KL. Constructive urban planning suggestion.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் பொதுப் போக்குவரத்து மேம்படுத்தப்பட வேண்டும். தினமும் ட்ராஃபிக் ஜாமில் மணிக்கணக்காக வீணாகிறது. அரசு கவனிக்க வேண்டும்.
reason: Calls for public transport improvement due to traffic congestion. Standard civic complaint in Tamil.

input_text: தமிழ்ப்பள்ளிகளுக்கு அரசு நிதி உதவி அதிகரிக்க வேண்டும். பல பள்ளிகளின் கட்டிடங்கள் பழுதடைந்துள்ளன. மாணவர்கள் பாதிக்கப்படுகிறார்கள்.
reason: Advocates for more government funding for Tamil schools. Legitimate education policy concern.

input_text: தீபாவளி, ஹரிராயா, சீன புத்தாண்டு - எல்லாப் பண்டிகைகளையும் ஒன்றாகக் கொண்டாடுவதுதான் மலேசியாவின் சிறப்பு.
reason: Celebrates Malaysia's multicultural festivals together. Promotes interfaith and intercultural harmony.

input_text: வீட்டு விலை மிகவும் அதிகமாகிவிட்டது. சாதாரண ஊழியர்களால் வீடு வாங்க முடியவில்லை. அரசு மலிவு விலை வீடுகளை கட்ட வேண்டும்.
reason: Complaint about housing affordability and call for affordable housing. Standard economic concern.

input_text: அரசு மருத்துவமனைகளில் காத்திருப்பு நேரம் மிக நீளமாக உள்ளது. மருத்துவர்கள் எண்ணிக்கையை அதிகரிக்க வேண்டும்.
reason: Critique of public hospital waiting times and call for more doctors. Health policy concern.

input_text: ஊழல் எந்தக் கட்சியாக இருந்தாலும் தண்டிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். SPRM சுதந்திரமாக செயல்பட வேண்டும்.
reason: Non-partisan call for anti-corruption enforcement and MACC independence. Civic integrity concern.

input_text: குறைந்தபட்ச ஊதியம் உயர்த்தப்பட வேண்டும். இன்றைய வாழ்க்கைச் செலவுக்கு RM1500 போதாது.
reason: Advocates for minimum wage increase relative to cost of living. Standard labor policy discussion.

input_text: மலேசியாவில் அமைதியான போராட்டம் நடத்துவது மக்களின் உரிமை. அரசியலமைப்பு அதற்கு உத்தரவாதம் அளிக்கிறது.
reason: Affirms constitutional right to peaceful protest. Democratic rights advocacy.

input_text: ரமலான் நேரத்தில் நம் முஸ்லிம் நண்பர்களை மதிப்போம். இது நம் மலேசிய கலாச்சாரத்தின் அழகு.
reason: Promotes respect for Muslim friends during Ramadan. Encourages intercultural courtesy.

input_text: சுற்றுச்சூழலை பாதுகாப்பது நம் எல்லோரின் பொறுப்பு. பிளாஸ்டிக் பயன்பாட்டைக் குறைக்க வேண்டும்.
reason: Environmental advocacy calling for reduced plastic use. Constructive sustainability message.

input_text: Honestly MRT bagus la tapi coverage still tak cukup. Nak pergi Subang dari KL kena tukar 3 kali. Government should expand lagi the network.
reason: Mixed BM-English feedback on MRT coverage. Constructive transport critique typical of Malaysian online discourse.

input_text: Harga groceries semua naik but gaji still sama je. Macam mana nak survive ni? Cost of living crisis is real bro.
reason: Code-switched complaint about cost of living vs stagnant wages. Natural Malaysian mixed-language expression.

input_text: I think dasar pendidikan kita need reform la. Too exam-oriented. Kids stressed out belajar for exam je, no critical thinking skills.
reason: Education policy critique in mixed language calling for less exam-focused system. Constructive suggestion.

input_text: Subsidy minyak tu kena target properly la. Orang drive Alphard sama subsidy dengan orang drive Kancil? Doesn't make sense kan.
reason: Argues for targeted fuel subsidies using relatable car examples. Standard policy debate in mixed language.

input_text: Best thing about Malaysia is our food diversity la. Nasi lemak, char kuey teow, roti canai, banana leaf rice - semua sedap. Ini yang unite kita.
reason: Celebrates Malaysian food diversity as a unifying cultural element. Positive social commentary.

input_text: Kerajaan cakap nak digital transformation tapi internet kat kampung still dial-up speed. Walk the talk la please.
reason: Criticism of government digital promises vs rural internet reality. Legitimate infrastructure complaint.

input_text: Lepas raya semua balik kampung, highway memang jam teruk. PLUS kena improve capacity la, bukan just raise toll every year.
reason: Criticism of highway operator raising tolls without improving capacity. Consumer complaint about transport.

input_text: Our healthcare workers are heroes la seriously. Underpaid, overworked, tapi still dedicated. Government kena treat them better.
reason: Appreciation for healthcare workers with call for better government treatment. Constructive advocacy.

input_text: Corruption tak kira party mana pun. SPRM should go after semua orang yang corrupt, regardless of political affiliation. Ini soal integrity negara.
reason: Non-partisan anti-corruption stance calling for equal enforcement. Civic integrity position.

input_text: Aku respect semua agama la. Ramadan ke, Thaipusam ke, Christmas ke - kita semua Malaysian at the end of the day. Unity in diversity betul tak?
reason: Expresses respect for all religions and Malaysian diversity. Promotes interfaith harmony.

input_text: Local council area aku memang fail la bab garbage collection. Every week ada skip day. Bayar cukai pintu for what kalau service macam ni?
reason: Frustration about unreliable garbage collection despite paying local taxes. Legitimate municipal service complaint.

input_text: Election coming soon and honestly I hope semua rakyat keluar mengundi. Jangan complain pasal kerajaan kalau you sendiri tak vote.
reason: Encourages voter turnout ahead of elections. Promotes democratic participation.

input_text: Housing affordability is a real crisis la kat KL. Rumah flat pun dah RM300k. How can fresh grad afford? Need more rumah mampu milik.
reason: Highlights housing affordability crisis for young workers. Calls for more affordable housing options.

input_text: Banjir kat Kelantan tiap tahun same story. Billions spent tapi masih flood. Where's the money going? Need proper accountability la.
reason: Questions spending on flood mitigation in Kelantan with call for accountability. Legitimate fiscal critique.

input_text: I think peaceful protest memang hak rakyat la. Selagi tak violent, tak kacau orang, it's a legitimate way to express dissatisfaction.
reason: Supports right to peaceful protest with explicit non-violence condition. Constitutional rights discussion.

input_text: The lack of cycling infrastructure in Malaysian cities is embarrassing. We talk about being green but there's not a single safe bike lane in most towns.
reason: Criticism of urban cycling infrastructure. Constructive environmental and transport policy feedback.

input_text: I appreciate that Malaysia has free primary and secondary education but the quality gap between urban and rural schools is massive. Every child deserves equal opportunity.
reason: Highlights urban-rural education quality gap while acknowledging existing policy. Constructive equity concern.

input_text: Malaysians from all backgrounds came together during the floods to help each other. That's the real Malaysia. Not the divisive politics you see online.
reason: Positive observation about cross-community solidarity during floods. Celebrates national unity.

input_text: SMEs are the backbone of our economy but getting bank loans is nearly impossible for small business owners. Government should simplify access to financing.
reason: Advocates for better SME financing access. Constructive economic policy suggestion.
